3. Scenario Structure

Each scenario: Location – Water – Planting window – Cultivar.
  • Water: RFD (rainfed) | IRRI (irrigated)
  • Planting window: ELY (early) | MID (mid) | LTE (late)
  • Cultivar: check (vpdtp=0) | LT1.5 / LT2 / LT2.5 (vpdtp=1)

Managements: AR-IRRI-ELY AR-IRRI-LTE AR-IRRI-MID AR-RFD-ELY AR-RFD-LTE AR-RFD-MID MO-IRRI-ELY MO-IRRI-LTE MO-IRRI-MID MO-RFD-ELY MO-RFD-LTE MO-RFD-MID NE-IRRI-ELY NE-IRRI-LTE NE-IRRI-MID NE-RFD-ELY NE-RFD-LTE NE-RFD-MID

Cultivars: Soybean-LT1.5-MG3 Soybean-LT1.5-MG4 Soybean-LT2-MG3 Soybean-LT2-MG4 Soybean-LT2.5-MG3 Soybean-LT2.5-MG4 Soybean-MG3 Soybean-MG4

8. Hourly VPD Analysis

Hourly VPD was computed for each simulation day using the same model equations (Spitters solar geometry, asymmetric sinusoidal temperature curve, Magnus–Tetens VP formula). Days were classified as vegetative (emergence → R3) or reproductive (R3 → R7) using the actual phenology of each simulation year. Mean profiles are averaged over all 30 simulation years (check cultivar).

The 2.0 kPa threshold (dotted line) corresponds to the VPDcr of the LT2 cultivar. Hours or days above this threshold drive stomatal restriction and DM production penalties in LT cultivars. Individual PNG files are saved to outputs/results/daily/.

Table: Cumulative hours with VPD > 2 kPa per season

Total daylight hours above 2 kPa accumulated over the vegetative (emergence → R3), reproductive (R3 → R7), and total (emergence → R7) phases, for each planting window. Values: mean ± SD across the 30 simulation years (check cultivar).
